rulings.Case Title: STEPHANIE CHU VS. JP MORGAN CHASE NATIONAL CORPORATE SERVICES, INC et al
rulings.Rulings: HOUSING COURT LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, APRIL 18, 2014. Line 6. DEFENDANT JPMORGAN CHASE BANK, N.A.'s DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT SUSTAINED. Demurer to wrongful foreclosure and violation of Rosenthal Act causes of action is sustained without leave to amend. No opposition. Demurrer to causes of action for breach of covenant of good faith, fraud, promissory estoppel and unfair competition is SUSTAINED with leave to amend to allege fraud with requisite particularity; to allege breach of covenant of good faith based on contract between parties, e.g. Note, DOT; to allege unambiguous promise and facts showing defendant's failure to perform under promise. Plaintiff may allege cause of action for negligence if she may do so in good faith. Prevailing party prepare order. =(501/LS)

rulings.Case Title: MONIQUE DE ROOVER VS. JACQUELINE P. YOUNG-DE ROOVER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Order Dismissing X-Complaint
rulings.Rulings: HOUSING COURT LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, APRIL 18, 2014. Line 7. CROSS DEFENDANT HEATHER HYRONS Motion For Order Dismissing X-Complaint GRANTED in part. First cause of action fro fraud is dismissed with prejudice as to defendant Hyrons only. Request for sanctions is denied. CCP 128.5 is not applicable to the compliant/proceedings initiated after December 31, 1994. Prevailing party prepare order. =(501/LS)

rulings.Case Title: YALE HOTEL VS. MICHAE LU MCCLOSKEUY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Amend The Judgment
rulings.Rulings: HOUSING COURT LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, APRIL 18, 2014. Line 13. PLAINTIFF YALE HOTEL, LLC's Motion To Amend The Judgment GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. rulings.Case Title: HOWARD L. WESTON VS. MIMI LAY et al
rulings.Rulings: HOUSING COURT LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, APRIL 18, 2014. Line 14. DEFENDANTs MIMI LAY and WILLIAM LAY's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT Overruled in part and sustained in part. Motion is sustained with leave to amend as to Defendant William Lay to properly allege service of "3 Day Notice". Demurrer is otherwise overruled. Complaint sufficiently alleges facts to allege compliance with Rent Ordinance. Defendants cite no authority for the proposition that the complete text of just cause per SFRO 37.9 has to be included in the "3 Day Notice." =(501/LS)
